title: Sarga 68
summary: Rama's divine birth, childhood, protection of Sage Vishwamitra's rituals from demons, breaking of Shiva's bow, and marriage to Sita. Addresses youth, childhood, education, overcoming early obstacles, and marriage.

sections.summary: Themes: mortality and immortality. The passage moves from “Having listened to their (the counsellors') words, Vasistha replied to the host of friends and counsellors of the king and to all the brahmins:” through “The family priest and all the counsellors wish your welfare. You must return in haste. There is an urgent task required to be done by you.” through “The messengers proceeded in westerly direction for some distance and then crossed the southern end of the Aparatala mountain. Thereafter, they…”

Themes: action and duty. The passage moves from “The messengers crossed Ganga at Hastinapura, and went in the westerly direction through the middle of Kurujangala and reached the country of…” through “There they saw the brahmins versed in the Vedas and drank water with cupped palms. Then they continued their journey through the middle of…” through “To please their master (sage Vasistha) and to preserve the royal dynasty and also to protect the honour of the (race and people), the messengers…”

verses.sanskrit: तेषां हि वचनं श्रुत्वा वसिष्ठः प्रत्युवाच ह। मित्रामात्यगणान्सर्वान्ब्राह्मणांस्तानिदं वचः।।2.68.1।।
verses.translation: Having listened to their (the counsellors') words, Vasistha replied to the host of friends and counsellors of the king and to all the brahmins:

verses.sanskrit: यदसौ मातुलकुले दत्तराज्यं परं सुखी। भरतो वसति भ्रात्रा शत्रुघ्नेन समन्वितः।।2.68.2।। तच्छीघ्रं जवना दूता गच्छन्तु त्वरितैर्हयैः। आनेतुं भ्रातरौ वीरौ किं समीक्षामहे वयम्।।2.68.3।।
verses.translation: Bharata on whom the kingdom has been conferred is living comfortably in his maternal uncle's house. Let swift messengers go quickly on speeding horses and bring back the valiant brothers, Bharata and Satrughna. What is there to deliberate for us in this matter?

verses.sanskrit: यदसौ मातुलकुले दत्तराज्यं परं सुखी। भरतो वसति भ्रात्रा शत्रुघ्नेन समन्वितः।।2.68.2।। तच्छीघ्रं जवना दूता गच्छन्तु त्वरितैर्हयैः। आनेतुं भ्रातरौ वीरौ किं समीक्षामहे वयम्।।2.68.3।।
verses.translation: Bharata on whom the kingdom has been conferred is living comfortably in his maternal uncle's house. Let swift messengers go quickly on speeding horses and bring back the valiant brothers, Bharata and Satrughna. What is there to deliberate for us in this matter?

verses.sanskrit: गच्छन्त्विति तत स्सर्वे वसिष्ठं वाक्यमब्रुवन्। तेषां तद्वचनं श्रूत्वा वसिष्ठो वाक्यमब्रवीत्।।2.68.4।।
verses.translation: Let the messengers go, said all of them to Vasistha. On hearing this, Vasistha replied again.

verses.sanskrit: एहि सिद्धार्थ विजय जयन्ताशोक नन्दन। श्रूयतामिति कर्तव्यं सर्वानेव ब्रवीमि वः।।2.68.5।।
verses.translation: Calling Siddhartha, Vijaya, Jayanta, Asoka and Nandana, Vasistha said, All of you listen to what is required to be done (by you).

verses.sanskrit: पुरं राजगृहं गत्वा शीघ्रं शीघ्रजवै र्हयैः। त्यक्तशोकैरिदं वाच्य श्शासनाद्भरतो मम।।2.68.6।।
verses.translation: Go at once from here on swift horses to the city of Rajagrha and concealing signs of grief, convey these words to Bharata as my command:

verses.sanskrit: पुरोहित स्त्वां कुशलं प्राह सर्वे च मन्त्रिणः। त्वरमाणश्च निर्याहि कृत्यमात्ययिकं त्वया।।2.68.7।।
verses.translation: The family priest and all the counsellors wish your welfare. You must return in haste. There is an urgent task required to be done by you.

verses.sanskrit: मा चास्मै प्रोषितं रामं मा चास्मै पितरं मृतम्। भवन्त श्शंसिषुर्गत्वा राघवाणामिमं क्षयम्।।2.68.8।।
verses.translation: All of you go there and do not communicate (to Bharata) about the exile of Rama or the demise of his father and the disaster that has befallen the Raghus.

verses.sanskrit: कौशेयानि च वस्त्राणि भूषणानि वराणि च। क्षिप्रमादय राज्ञश्च भरतस्य च गच्छत।।2.68.9।।
verses.translation: Take with you silk garments and precious ornaments for the king of Kekaya and Bharata and depart at once.

verses.sanskrit: दत्तपथ्यशना दूता जग्मुस्स्वं स्वं निवेशनम्। केकयां स्ते गमिष्यन्तो हयानारुह्य संमतान्।।2.68.10।।
verses.translation: The messengers set to depart to Kekaya kingdom, carrying with them the necessaries for their journey went to their respective homes mounted on excellent horses.

verses.sanskrit: ततः प्रास्थानिकं कृत्वा कार्यशेषमनन्तरम्। वसिष्ठेनाभ्यनुज्ञाता दूता स्संत्वरिता ययुः।।2.68.11।।
verses.translation: Having completed the rest of the arrangements for the journey and permitted by Vasistha, the messengers left.

verses.sanskrit: न्यन्तेनापरतालस्य प्रलम्बस्योत्तरं प्रति। निषेवमाणा स्ते जग्मुर्नदीं मध्येन मालिनीम्।।2.68.12।।
verses.translation: The messengers proceeded in westerly direction for some distance and then crossed the southern end of the Aparatala mountain. Thereafter, they travelled northward along the bank of Malini that flows in between Aparatala and Pralamba mountains.

verses.sanskrit: ते हस्तिनापुरे गङ्गां तीर्त्वा प्रत्यङ्मुखा ययुः। पाञ्चालदेशमासाद्य मध्येन कुरुजाङ्गलम्।।2.68.13।। सरांसि च सुपूर्णानि नदीश्च विमलोदकाः। निरीक्षमाणा स्ते जग्मुर्दूताः कार्यवशाद्द्रुतम्।।2.68.14।।
verses.translation: The messengers crossed Ganga at Hastinapura, and went in the westerly direction through the middle of Kurujangala and reached the country of Panchala. Beholding lakes full of water and rivers of pellucid waters there, they travelled fast with a view to fulfilling their mission.

verses.sanskrit: ते हस्तिनापुरे गङ्गां तीर्त्वा प्रत्यङ्मुखा ययुः। पाञ्चालदेशमासाद्य मध्येन कुरुजाङ्गलम्।।2.68.13।। सरांसि च सुपूर्णानि नदीश्च विमलोदकाः। निरीक्षमाणा स्ते जग्मुर्दूताः कार्यवशाद्द्रुतम्।।2.68.14।।
verses.translation: The messengers crossed Ganga at Hastinapura, and went in the westerly direction through the middle of Kurujangala and reached the country of Panchala. Beholding lakes full of water and rivers of pellucid waters there, they travelled fast with a view to fulfilling their mission.

verses.sanskrit: ते प्रसन्नोदकां दिव्यां नानाविहगसेविताम्। उपातिजग्मुर्वेगेन शरदण्डां जनाकुलाम्।।2.68.15।।
verses.translation: The messengers crossed the divine Saradanda of calm waters frequented by fowls of various speices and thronged with people. And proceeded speedily.

verses.sanskrit: निकूलवृक्षमासाद्य दिव्यं सत्योपयाचनम्। अभिगम्याभिवाद्यं तं कुलिङ्गां प्राविशन्पुरीम्।।2.68.16।।
verses.translation: They reached a divine tree (of great powers) known as Satyopayachana tree and having gone round and paid homage they entered the Kulinga city.

verses.sanskrit: आभिकालं ततः प्राप्य ते बोधिभवनाच्च्युताम्। पितृपैतामहीं पुण्यां तेरुरिक्षुमतीं नदीम्।।2.68.17।।
verses.translation: From there they reached a village known as Abhikala and crossed the sacred Ikshumati flowing from Bodhibhavana mountain. This region was enjoyed by fathers and forefathers of Dasaratha.

verses.sanskrit: अवेक्ष्याञ्जलिपानांश्च ब्राह्मणान्वेदपारगान्। ययुर्मध्येन बाह्लीकान् सुदामानं च पर्वतम्।।2.68.18।।
verses.translation: There they saw the brahmins versed in the Vedas and drank water with cupped palms. Then they continued their journey through the middle of Bahlika country, and reached Sudama mountain.

verses.sanskrit: विष्णोः पदं प्रेक्षमाणा विपाशांचापि शाल्मलीम्। नदीर्वापी स्तटाकानि पल्वलानि सरांसि च।।2.68.19।। पश्यन्तो विविधांश्चापि सिंहाव्याघ्रमृग द्विपान्। ययुः पथाऽतिमहता शासनं भर्तुरीप्सवः।।2.68.20।।
verses.translation: Beholding the footprints of Visnu (on Sudama mountain), and seeing Vipasa, and Salmali rivers, trees, streams, deep wells, ponds, pools, large tanks, different kinds of lions, tigers and elephants, they travelled a long distance with the intention of fulfilling the command of their master.

verses.sanskrit: विष्णोः पदं प्रेक्षमाणा विपाशांचापि शाल्मलीम्। नदीर्वापी स्तटाकानि पल्वलानि सरांसि च।।2.68.19।। पश्यन्तो विविधांश्चापि सिंहाव्याघ्रमृग द्विपान्। ययुः पथाऽतिमहता शासनं भर्तुरीप्सवः।।2.68.20।।
verses.translation: Beholding the footprints of Visnu (on Sudama mountain), and seeing Vipasa, and Salmali rivers, trees, streams, deep wells, ponds, pools, large tanks, different kinds of lions, tigers and elephants, they travelled a long distance with the intention of fulfilling the command of their master.

verses.sanskrit: ते श्रान्तवाहना दूता विकृष्णेन पथा ततः। गिरिव्रजं पुरवरं शीघ्रमासेदुरञ्जसा।।2.68.21।।
verses.translation: After a prolonged journey the messengers with their tired horses soon reached Girivraja, the (capital city of Kekaya kingdom) best of cities.

verses.sanskrit: भर्तुः प्रियार्थं कुलरक्षणार्थं भर्तुश्च वंशस्य परिग्रहार्थम्। अहेडमाना स्त्वरया स्म दूता रात्र्यान्तु ते तत्पुरमेव याताः।।2.68.22।।
verses.translation: To please their master (sage Vasistha) and to preserve the royal dynasty and also to protect the honour of the (race and people), the messengers soon reached their destination by night without any negligence of their duty. इत्यार्षे श्रीमद्रामायणे वाल्मीकीय आदिकाव्ये अयोध्याकाण्डे अष्टषष्टितमस्सर्गः।। Thus ends the sixtyeighth sarga in Ayodhyakanda of the holy Ramayana, the first epic composed by sage Valmiki.
